phases.title: Hangar Floor, Cibubur
phases.body: PKL at a helicopter MRO shop. Started in January, wrapped the hands-on work by early March, fully signed off in April. Bell, Sikorsky, Airbus on the floor. This was before any crypto, and it is still where I felt most at home.

phases.title: Into Crypto, Straight to Lead
phases.body: Joined crypto at the end of November after watching a friend hit an Optimism airdrop. Almost immediately took a mod-lead role at Vitall on Solana. No slow ramp, thrown straight into the deep end.

phases.title: Learning the Controls
phases.body: Started with Telegram airdrops, then actually learned the craft: testnets, revoking approvals, governance votes, wallet hygiene. Mid-year I took my first swing at a validator. Pure pain, zero results, just study. By late 2021 it clicked, and the projects I touched started to pay off.

phases.title: Aptos Validator, ~2,200 APTOS
phases.body: Tried validators again in a dead bear market. Quiet and discouraging, but I kept grinding and stood up an Aptos validator. In October I became eligible and earned about 2,200 APTOS. I rolled that into running more nodes and farming Arbitrum retroactive with the capital.

phases.title: The Big Wins
phases.body: March brought the Aptos airdrop, worth around 90M IDR. In April and May I locked $45 into a SUI pre-order on Bybit and qualified for 1,300 SUI. My best stretch. It also opened with a small 500K IDR drain on my Arbitrum wallet, and by year end the drainers were back and persistent.

phases.title: The $4K Hit
phases.body: First day of Ramadan in April: about 2,200 WORMHOLE, worth roughly $4K, gone to an approval phish. Then I missed LayerZero in May and zkSync in June. October returned a small $86 Optimism reward. A year that mostly took.

phases.title: Dry Season, Then Freelance
phases.body: Early 2025 was bone dry. I poured everything into my portfolio and it still felt like a total wipeout. Mid-year I signed up on Upwork and Fiverr. In November I landed my first Fiverr client, Daniel: $150 to set up 8 nodes that turned out to be full nodes, not reward validators. He wanted free fixes. I quoted $9,900 to do it properly with docs, and he walked. ($35 of mine is still stuck on Pioneer, below the $50 withdrawal floor.)

phases.title: AI Agents & Bug Bounty
phases.body: The hardest stretch yet, and still ongoing. But at the end of May I found AI agents and bug bounty, only about a month ago, and it has been the fastest I have ever learned anything. New heading, engines still running.
